info.title: Securonix SIEM REST API
info.description: Securonix Unified Defense SIEM REST API for incident operations, asset searches, and security analytics.

get.description: Retrieve incident details, status, workflow, possible actions, child cases, activity history, or list incidents by date range. Send 'Accept: application/vnd.snypr.app-v6.0+json' to receive the full enriched response payload (without it, list/metaInfo responses are returned in a reduced legacy format). Per Securonix docs: v2.0+json is the documented Accept for type=metaInfo, v3.0+json for type=list, v6.0+json works for both and is recommended.

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: Versioned response media type. Use 'application/vnd.snypr.app-v6.0+json' for the richest response. Omit (or use '*/*') only if the deployment rejects versioned Accept headers.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The type of incident information to retrieve. metaInfo: full incident details. status: incident status only. workflow: workflow name for an incident. workflows: list all available workflows. workflowDetails: details for a named workflow (use with workflowname). defaultAssignee: default assignee for a workflow (use with workflow). actions: possible actions for an incident. actionInfo: check if a specific action is available (use with actionName). childCaseInfo: child case IDs for a parent incident. activityStreamInfo: case activity history. list: incidents within a date range (requires from, to, rangeType).

parameters.name: incidentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the incident. Required when type is metaInfo, status, workflow, actions, actionInfo, childCaseInfo, or activityStreamInfo.

parameters.name: workflowname
parameters.description: Workflow name. Required when type is workflowDetails.

parameters.name: workflow
parameters.description: Workflow name. Required when type is defaultAssignee.

parameters.name: actionName
parameters.description: Action name to check. Required when type is actionInfo.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start time in epoch milliseconds. Required when type is 'list'.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End time in epoch milliseconds. Required when type is 'list'.

parameters.name: rangeType
parameters.description: The type of date range filter. Required when type is 'list'.

parameters.name: max
parameters.description: Maximum number of incidents to return. Used when type is 'list'.

parameters.name: tenantname
parameters.description: The name of the tenant (MSSP). Used when type is 'list'.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Sort order for the results. Used when type is 'list'.

200.description: Incident details or list of incidents

post.description: Perform an action on an incident. Use 'Possible Actions Available for an Incident' API (type=actions) to view available actions. For general actions, provide incidentId and actionName. To update incident criticality, set actionName to 'ChangeIncidentCriticality' and provide criticality, changecriticality, and bulkcaseids parameters.

parameters.name: actionName
parameters.description: The action to perform on the incident. Use 'ChangeIncidentCriticality' to update criticality. Run the Available Threat Actions API to view other available actions. This parameter is case sensitive.

parameters.name: incidentId
parameters.description: The incident ID on which you want to perform an action. Required for general incident actions (not required for ChangeIncidentCriticality).

parameters.name: criticality
parameters.description: The criticality level to set. Required when actionName is 'ChangeIncidentCriticality'.

parameters.name: changecriticality
parameters.description: Must be set to 'true' when actionName is 'ChangeIncidentCriticality'. This parameter is case sensitive.

parameters.name: bulkcaseids
parameters.description: Incident ID(s) to update criticality for. For multiple incidents, repeat this parameter (e.g. bulkcaseids=id1&bulkcaseids=id2). Required when actionName is 'ChangeIncidentCriticality'.

get.description: Universal Spotter search endpoint. Use this single endpoint for every index type — activity, violation, asset, lookup, watchlist — by setting the 'index=<type>' clause inside the query string. There are NO per-index sub-endpoints; all searches go here. Pipe (|) operator searches and OR operators are not supported. Best practice for activity searches: limit each call to 7 days.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Spotter query string. STRICT SYNTAX RULES — every rule below has caused a real 400. (1) Query MUST begin with index=<type> where <type> is one of: activity, violation, asset, lookup, watchlist. (2) Multiple conditions MUST be joined with the keyword and (or AND). Never use commas, semicolons, or whitespace alone — Securonix returned 400 on a query that read: index=violation policyname="X" ipaddress=Y because and was missing between clauses. (3) STRING LITERALS MUST USE DOUBLE QUOTES — the character " — and never single quotes ('). Single quotes are the #1 cause of 400 errors here: passing accountname = 'johndoe' returns 400; you must pass accountname = "johndoe". This applies even when the string has no spaces. (4) Put a space on each side of =. (5) Attribute names are lowercase — use tenantname (not tenant or @tenantName). The leading @ on attribute names is optional and may be omitted; if you do use it, lowercase it. (6) Common attributes by index: activity → resourcegroupname, accountname, ipaddress, transactionstring, tenantname; violation → policyname, ipaddress, resourcegroupname, accountname, tenantname; asset → entityname, tenantname; lookup → lookupname, key; watchlist → watchlistname, entityname. ----- WRONG vs RIGHT pairs (study these before composing a query) ----- WRONG (single quotes, 400): index=activity and accountname = 'johndoe' and tenantname = 'Acme' --- RIGHT: index=activity and accountname = "johndoe" and tenantname = "Acme" ----- WRONG (no and between clauses, 400): index=violation policyname="X" ipaddress="1.2.3.4" --- RIGHT: index=violation and policyname = "X" and ipaddress = "1.2.3.4" ----- WRONG (eventtime params with index=violation, 400): query: index=violation and policyname = "X" with eventtime_from=...&eventtime_to=... --- RIGHT: same query but pass generationtime_from / generationtime_to instead — violation searches DO NOT accept eventtime_*. ----- More correct examples — activity: index=activity and resourcegroupname = "Cloud-IDC-O365-OUTLOOK" and tenantname = "Acme" --- violation: index=violation and policyname = "Abnormal Number of Emails to Nonbusiness Domains" and accountname = "johndoe@example.com" and resourcegroupname = "Cloud-IDC-O365-OUTLOOK" and tenantname = "Acme" (and pass generationtime_from / generationtime_to, NOT eventtime_*) --- lookup: index=lookup and lookupname = "VulnerableHostLookUpTable" --- watchlist: index=watchlist and watchlistname = "Flight Risk Users" --- asset: index=asset and entityname = "ASSET-EXAMPLE|12345_67890"

parameters.name: eventtime_from
parameters.description: Event time start range in format MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm:ss. Use this ONLY when index=activity. DO NOT pass eventtime_from when index=violation — violation queries with eventtime_from return HTTP 400; use generationtime_from instead. Omit entirely for index=asset, index=lookup, and index=watchlist.

parameters.name: eventtime_to
parameters.description: Event time end range in format MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm:ss. Use this ONLY when index=activity. DO NOT pass eventtime_to when index=violation — violation queries with eventtime_to return HTTP 400; use generationtime_to instead. Omit entirely for index=asset, index=lookup, and index=watchlist.

parameters.name: generationtime_from
parameters.description: Violation generation time start range in format MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm:ss. REQUIRED when index=violation. DO NOT also pass eventtime_from in the same call — pick one time-range pair based on the index. Omit entirely for index=activity, index=asset, index=lookup, and index=watchlist.

parameters.name: generationtime_to
parameters.description: Violation generation time end range in format MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm:ss. REQUIRED when index=violation. DO NOT also pass eventtime_to in the same call — pick one time-range pair based on the index. Omit entirely for index=activity, index=asset, index=lookup, and index=watchlist.

parameters.name: tz
parameters.description: Timezone. If empty, the application timezone will be selected.

parameters.name: prettyJson
parameters.description: If set to true, returns the REST response in pretty JSON format. Default: false.

parameters.name: max
parameters.description: Number of records to return. Default: 1000, Max: 10000. Only available for version 6.2 CU4 SP4 and above.

parameters.name: queryId
parameters.description: Used for pagination. Use the queryId from the previous response to get the next set of records. Only available for version 6.2 CU4 SP4 and above.

parameters.name: data_labels
parameters.description: If set to true, returns data dictionary labels instead of Securonix attributes. Default: true.

post.description: Create a new watchlist. Watchlists are used for keeping a close watch on entities based on inherent risks. This API is only available for version 6.2 CU4 SP5 and above.

parameters.name: watchlistname
parameters.description: The name of the watchlist that you want to create.

parameters.name: tenantname
parameters.description: The name of the tenant where the watchlist will be created (MSSP).

get.description: List all existing watchlists. This API is only available for version 6.2 CU4 SP5 and above.

parameters.name: tenantname
parameters.description: The name of the tenant whose watchlists will be listed (MSSP).

get.summary: Check if an Entity is Watchlisted
get.description: Check if an entity is part of a watchlist. This API is only available for version 6.2 CU4 SP5 and above.

parameters.name: entityId
parameters.description: The entity ID. The entityId is employeeid when entitytype is Users.

parameters.name: watchlistname
parameters.description: The watchlist name to check if the entity is a part of. If not specified, returns all watchlists the entity is part of.

parameters.name: tenantname
parameters.description: The name of the tenant where you want to check if the entity is watchlisted (MSSP).

post.description: Add one or more entities to an existing watchlist. Maximum 5 comma-separated entities. This API is only available for version 6.2 CU4 SP5 and above.

parameters.name: watchlistname
parameters.description: The watchlist name to add the entity.

parameters.name: entityType
parameters.description: The type of the entity (Users, Activityaccount, Resource, Activityip).

parameters.name: expirydays
parameters.description: Number of days the entity will be assigned to the watchlist.

parameters.name: entityId
parameters.description: The entity ID. Maximum 5 comma-separated entities. The entityId is employeeid when entitytype is Users.

parameters.name: resourcegroupid
parameters.description: The resource group ID of the entity. Specify -1 for entitytype Users.

parameters.name: tenantname
parameters.description: The name of the tenant where you want to add an entity to a watchlist (MSSP).

get.description: List all entities present in an existing watchlist. This API is only available for version 6.2 CU4 SP5 and above.

parameters.name: watchlistname
parameters.description: The watchlist name to list all entities.

parameters.name: tenantname
parameters.description: The name of the tenant where the watchlist is available.

get.description: Check which watchlists one or more entities are part of. This API is only available for version 6.2 CU4 SP5 and above.

parameters.name: entityId
parameters.description: The entity ID (comma-separated). The entityId is employeeid when the entitytype is Users.

parameters.name: tenantname
parameters.description: The name of the tenant where the watchlist is available (MSSP).

post.description: Create a new global whitelist. Whitelists allow you to create lists of entities to exclude from policies.

parameters.name: whitelistname
parameters.description: The name of the whitelist to create.

parameters.name: tenantname
parameters.description: The name of the tenant the whitelist should belong to.

parameters.name: entitytype
parameters.description: The type of entity that the whitelist is intended to hold (Users, Activityaccount, Resources, IpAddress).

delete.description: Remove a value from an existing whitelist. Can be used for both Global and Attribute whitelists.

parameters.name: whitelistname
parameters.description: The name of the whitelist to remove the value from.

parameters.name: tenantname
parameters.description: The name of the tenant the whitelist belongs to.

parameters.name: whitelisttype
parameters.description: The type of whitelist (Global or Attribute).

parameters.name: entityId
parameters.description: The entityId value to remove (employeeId for Users, accountname for ActivityAccount, resourcename for Resources, ipadress for IpAddress). Required if whitelisttype is Global.

parameters.name: attributename
parameters.description: The name of the attribute being removed. Required if whitelisttype is Attribute. Examples: accountname, transactionstring, sourcetype.

parameters.name: attributevalue
parameters.description: The value of the attribute being removed. Required if whitelisttype is Attribute.

post.summary: Add Entity or Attribute to Whitelist
post.description: Add an entity or attribute to an existing whitelist.

get.description: Get a list of all existing whitelists.

parameters.name: tenantname
parameters.description: The name of the tenant the whitelist belongs to.

parameters.name: whitelistname
parameters.description: The name of the whitelist to list.

parameters.name: entitytype
parameters.description: The type of entity (Users, Activityaccount, Activityip, Resources).

get.description: List all entities present in a whitelist.

parameters.name: whitelistname
parameters.description: The name of the whitelist to list entities from.

parameters.name: tenantname
parameters.description: The name of the tenant the whitelist belongs to.
